steps.title: Write the perimeter formula
steps.content: Perimeter of a rectangle: \(P = 2L + 2W\). With length \(50\) and width \(x\): \(P = 2(50) + 2x = 100 + 2x\).

steps.title: Translate the constraint
steps.content:
“At most 210 inches” means the perimeter is less than or equal to 210: \(2x + 100 \le 210\).
  • A (\(2x + 100 \le 210\)): Matches both the perimeter expression and the ≤ constraint. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B (\(2x + 100 \ge 210\)): Uses \(\ge\), meaning “at least” — wrong direction.
  • C (\(2x + 50 \le 210\)): Constant should be \(2 \times 50 = 100\), not 50 — wrong coefficient.
  • D (\(2x + 50 \ge 210\)): Both the constant and the inequality direction are wrong.
